title: Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud | Documentation
content:
# Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud ## [Installation requirements](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#requirements "Installation requirements")
CategoryRequirement
Azure DevOps

The extension will work with:

  • Azure DevOps Services
  • Azure DevOps Server 2022 Update 2
  • Azure DevOps Server 2022 Update 1
  • Azure DevOps Server 2022 Update 0.1 Patch 3 and later
  • Azure DevOps Server 2020 Update 1.2 Patch 9 and later
  • Azure DevOps Server 2020 Update 0.2 Patch 4 and later
  • Azure DevOps Server 2019 Update 1.2 Patch 5 and later
  • Azure DevOps Server 2019 - patch 15 and later
Azure pipeline agents

The extension will work with all of the hosted agents (Windows, Linux, and macOS):

  • If you are using Microsoft-hosted agents, there is nothing else to install. 
  • If you are self-hosting the agents, check that at least the minimal version of Java supported by SonarQube Cloud is installed.
    In addition, make sure the appropriate build tools are installed on the agent for the type of project you are analyzing. For example, .NET Framework v4.6.2+/NET Core 3.1+ if building using MSBuild, Maven for Java projects, etc.

The minimum agent version for @3 tasks of the Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud is 3.218.0.

Allowed websites

In order to download binaries and communicate with SonarQube Cloud, the following URLs should be whitelisted:

  • sonarcloud.io
  • If using the Maven/Gradle mode or not using the default version of SonarScanner for .NET or CLI: the SonarSource binaries site (binaries.sonarsource.com).
## [Installing the extension](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#installing-extension "Installing the extension") 1. Sign in to your Azure DevOps Services organization or Azure DevOps Server collection with the dedicated technical account you created in [Importing your Azure DevOps organization](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/administering-sonarcloud/creating-organization/importing-azure-devops-organization/ "Importing your Azure DevOps organization"). 2. From the Visual Studio Marketplace, install the [Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SonarSource.sonarcloud "Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud") by selecting the **Get it free** button. ## [If upgrading from a previous version of the extension](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#upgrading-from-previous-version "If upgrading from a previous version of the extension") ### [Smooth migration](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#smooth-migration "Smooth migration") The v3 extension embeds the latest version of SonarScanner for .NET and SonarScanner CLI. However, to allow a smooth migration, you can set up your Azure build pipeline to use a previous version of one of these scanners and thus, continue using a previous SonarQube tasks version until you’re ready to upgrade. See [Using a specific version of SonarScanner for .NET or CLI](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/azure-pipelines/adding-analysis-to-build-pipeline/various-features/ "Using a specific version of SonarScanner for .NET or CLI"). In that case, the SonarSource binaries site (`binaries.sonarsource.com`) must be whitelisted. ### [Prepare analysis configuration task: new scanner mode values](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#prepare-analysis-configuration-task-new-scanner-mode-values "Prepare analysis configuration task: new scanner mode values") Allowable values for the `scannerMode` required property of the [**Prepare Analysis Configuration** task](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/azure-pipelines/sonarqube-tasks/ "Prepare Analysis Configuration task") have changed with the v7 extension. Please use the following in your @3 tasks: - `dotnet` for the .NET mode - `cli` for the CLI mode - `other` for the Maven / Gradle mode ### [Deprecation notices](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#deprecation-notices "Deprecation notices") Version @2 tasks were deprecated in v3.0 and will be dropped in a subsequent release. ## [Previous versions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#previous-versions "Previous versions") As new scanner versions are released, previous requirements and/or deprecations will be listed here. #### [Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud v2.2.x](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#azure-devops-extension-for-sonarqube-cloud-v22x "Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud v2.2.x") Because the current versions of the [SonarScanner for .NET](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarscanner-for-dotnet/installing/ "SonarScanner for .NET") or [SonarScanner CLI](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarscanner-cli/ "SonarScanner CLI") scanners are embedded and depending on your configuration, some additional setup may be required. - The SonarScanner for .NET has a new parameter for scanning multiple languages. The [Multi-language analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarscanner-for-dotnet/configuring/#multi-language-analysis "Multi-language analysis") article has full details. - If you want to specify the exact .NET or CLI scanner version, use the `msBuildVersion` and `cliVersion` properties. Please check the [Using the Prepare Analysis Configuration task](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#using-the-prepare-analysis-configuration-task "Using the Prepare Analysis Configuration task") article below for details. When specifying a particular scanner version, internet access is required by the pipelines calling the .NET or CLI scanners: - Access to [github.com](http://github.com/ "github.com") is required to download previous versions of the [SonarScanner for .NET](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarscanner-for-dotnet/installing/ "SonarScanner for .NET"). If you are white-listing [sonarcloud.io](http://sonarcloud.io/ "sonarcloud.io"), GitHub and its HTTP redirect, `objects.githubusercontent.com`, should also be whitelisted. - Access to [binaries.sonarsource.com](http://binaries.sonarsource.com/ "binaries.sonarsource.com") is required to download previous versions of the [SonarScanner CLI](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner/ "SonarScanner CLI"). If you are white-listing [sonarcloud.io](http://sonarcloud.io/ "sonarcloud.io"), the Sonar binaries should also be whitelisted. For users running on-premise or using self-hosted agents, the minimum agent version for SonarCloud v2 tasks is 3.218.0. #### [in v2.0.1](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#in-v201 "in v2.0.1") - Version @1 tasks were deprecated and will be dropped in a subsequent release. #### [Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud v1.x.x](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/ci-based-analysis/sonarcloud-extension-for-azure-devops/#azure-devops-extension-for-sonarqube-cloud-v1xx "Azure DevOps extension for SonarQube Cloud v1.x.x") Version @1 tasks were deprecated in v2.0.1 and will be dropped in a subsequent release. From version 1.0 of the Azure DevOps extension, the extension was fully rewritten in Node.js which means that analyses can be triggered on Linux and macOS agents. The mono dependency was dropped in version 1.3; this is not possible when using previous versions of the extension. For users running on-premise or using self-hosted agents, the minimum agent version for SonarCloud v1 tasks is 2.114.0.

headers.content-security-policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://app.kontent.ai https://downloads.sonarsource.com; base-uri 'self'; default-src data: 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' https:; script-src 'unsafe-inline' https:; style-src data: 'unsafe-inline' https:; img-src data: https: blob:; font-src data: https:; connect-src https: wss: blob:; media-src https: blob:; object-src ; child-src https: data: blob:; form-action 'self' https:; block-all-mixed-content
